ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Bilam bilam ti Yezu baŋɛ ti paŋa ze'ele a zi'an yehe. Ti a ŋmɛreha a mɛŋa nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan kaara gee sɔkera yeti, “Ani en kalum n fuo wa?”
translated: Jesus went to the synagogue on the Sabbath day as usual. When he stood up to read, they handed him the book of the prophet Isaiah.

ref: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
text: Gee ti pɔge sa'ara pia la ihege zomehe ba butolehi.
translated: The child is afraid to go to the farm because of the snakes there.

ref: Jesus touched their eyes and said, “Because of your faith, you will be healed.”
text: Ti Yezu kalum ba nini la gee yeti, “Ya sakerɛ en de se'em la, la tum bala mɛa bo' ya.”
translated: Jesus said to them, "If you believe in me, you will have eternal life."

ref: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
text: Ban paaɛ zamehegɔ yire nɛŋa daana wa yire la, a nyɛ la kɔ'ɔm ana saii zi'an woo, ti nɛreba kaaha gee fabela vɔɔla.
translated: When the child is born and grows up, he does not know what he will become, but if he is taught well, he will become a good person.

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Se'em woo n tuni Yinɛ sumboolum la, eŋa daana en de mam suuɔ, la mam ma.
translated: When the child saw the Yinɛ symbol, he immediately went to his mother and told her.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɛrɔd n ta baŋɛ ti baŋereba la pã'ahɛ en mɛ la, a ukɛ mɛ kankaŋi, gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba doohe kɔrum bibudibeto sɛba woo n boi Bɛtelehɛm la ka kɔrɔmkɔrɔm za'a, kɔmsɛba n de yuuma ayi' bii sɛba n pun ka paaɛ bala, doohe la hansɛka ti a wum ti baŋereba duma la tɔgɛ bo' en la.
translated: Herod secretly called the wise men and found out from them the exact time the star had appeared. He sent them to Bethlehem and said, "Go and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, report to me, so that I too may go and worship him."

text: “Nɛra kan ta'am tuna bɔ'ɔra kpɛ'ɛnduma bayi', se'ere en sɔi la, a wan ba'afum yaaɛ ayima gee nɔŋɛ ayima, bii a ba' tuna bɔ'ɔra ayima gee bahera ayima. Ya kan ta'am dɛna yameŋa bo' Yinɛ la lɔgerɔ buyina. Da fabele ya
translated: "If you don't listen to your parents' advice, you will suffer. If you listen to them, they will guide you well and you will succeed in life. You must listen to the voice of God in your heart."

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Bala, mam han tara Beelezebule sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, gee yamam nɛreba la me tari la ani sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi? Bala zuo, ya mɛhi zahereba la en wan gakɛ ya.
translated: But, if I have cast out Beelzebub by the finger of God, then has the kingdom of God come upon you? But if I cast out demons by Beelzebub, by whom do your sons cast them out?

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Ti nɔtɔgeheba pumparenduma zo'e zo'e wan wa'am na pa'ahɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e ti ba.
translated: The little girl is running very fast, but she cannot catch up to them.

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Ti la iŋɛ fii wa ti nɛreba la n ze' la paaɛ Pita zi'an yele en yeti, “Yelemɛŋerɛ fum dela ba ayima. Fum tugum n pa'alɛ.”
translated: When they arrived at the house, they saw that Peter had already come and was saying, “I have not come to fight. Fighting is not good.”
